data.name: A Fresh Start
data.description: Garak, a weathered veteran at the Hearthstead Inn, has offered to help you get started. Equip yourself at the local merchants and clear the vermin from the trail loop.
data.completedDialogue: Garak nods approvingly. "You've already proven yourself. Keep training and I'll have more work for you soon."

steps.description: Speak with {npc}Garak{/} at the {location}Hearthstead Inn{/} and ask about {yellow}opportunities{/}.
steps.completionDialogue: Garak looks you over with a practiced eye. "New in town, eh? You look like you could handle yourself, but not dressed like that. Head west to {npc}Perguth{/} at {location}Perguth's Training Weapons{/}. Tell him you need {yellow}training weapons{/}. He'll set you right. If you wield magic, seek out {npc}Zifnab's Apprentice{/} or {npc}Sister Althea{/} on the main street before you head out."

steps.description: Visit {npc}Perguth{/} at {location}Perguth's Training Weapons{/} and ask about {yellow}training weapons{/}.
steps.completionDialogue: Perguth nods and gestures to the rack behind him. "Aye, Garak sent you? Good man. Take a look at what I've got. Type {yellow}list{/} to see my stock, then {yellow}buy{/} what suits you." He leans on the counter. "Once you're armed, go see {npc}Geelee{/} next door east for {yellow}training armor{/}. No sense heading out unprotected."
steps.inProgressDialogue: "Still here? Go on, type {yellow}list{/} and pick something."

steps.description: Visit {npc}Geelee{/} at {location}Geelee's Training Armor{/} and ask about {yellow}training armor{/}.
steps.completionDialogue: Geelee sizes you up with a quick glance. "Perguth sent you, did he? Smart. The trail outside is crawling with rats and snakes. You'll want some padding before you tangle with them." She taps the display. "Type {yellow}list{/} and grab what you can afford. When you're geared up, head south to the {location}trail loop{/} and thin out the vermin. {npc}Garak{/} wants to see you clear out {yellow}10 rats{/} and {yellow}10 garter snakes{/}."
steps.inProgressDialogue: "Need more gear? Type {yellow}list{/} to see what I've got."

steps.description: Kill 10 rats on the {location}Hearthstead Loop{/} trail.
steps.inProgressDialogue: "Still got rats to clear? Keep at it."

steps.description: Kill 10 garter snakes on the {location}Hearthstead Loop{/} trail.
steps.inProgressDialogue: "Snakes too. Don't come back until they're dealt with."

steps.description: Return to {npc}Garak{/} at the {location}Hearthstead Inn{/} and tell him you're {yellow}done{/}.
steps.completionDialogue: Garak grins and slides a small pouch across the table. "Well done. The farmers will sleep easier tonight." He leans back. "You've got some fight in you. When you've gained enough experience, head to the back room and talk to {npc}Toren{/} about {yellow}training{/}. He can help you grow stronger. Once you've trained up, come see me again. I may have {yellow}more work{/} for you."
steps.inProgressDialogue: "The trail still needs clearing. Get to it."

data.name: Trouble in the Wilds
data.description: Garak has heard reports of something disturbing in the forest north of the trail loop. Investigate the Hearthstead Wilds and find out what is lurking there.
data.denialDialogue: Garak glances at you. "You're not ready for this yet. Come back when you've proven yourself on the trail loop."
data.completedDialogue: Garak nods. "You've already done more than most. The wilds are safer because of you."

steps.description: Speak with {npc}Garak{/} at the {location}Hearthstead Inn{/} and ask about {yellow}more work{/}.
steps.completionDialogue: Garak lowers his voice. "Something's been stirring in the forest north of the loop. Farmers are finding tracks they don't recognize, and a trapper went missing last week." He traces a path on the table with his finger. "Head to the trail loop and look for an {location}overgrown clearing{/}. There's a hidden path heading north. Type {yellow}go path{/} to follow it into the {location}Hearthstead Wilds{/}. Push through and look for anything unusual. Be careful; the creatures out there are tougher than the vermin on the loop."

steps.description: Kill 8 timber rats in the {location}Hearthstead Wilds{/}.
steps.inProgressDialogue: "The wilds are still dangerous. Keep at it."

steps.description: Explore the {location}Hearthstead Wilds{/} and investigate the {location}Abandoned Campsite{/}.
steps.completionDialogue: The campsite is a mess. A collapsed tent lies half-buried in fallen leaves, and scattered supplies are strewn across the ground. Near the cold fire pit, you find a {item}torn journal{/} wedged under a rock. The last entry is scrawled in a shaking hand: "Something in the cave to the north. Not animals. They took my pack and nearly took my life. Green skin, yellow eyes. Do not go in alone."

steps.description: Return the {item}torn journal{/} to {npc}Garak{/} at the {location}Hearthstead Inn{/} and tell him about the {yellow}journal{/}.
steps.completionDialogue: Garak reads the journal, his expression darkening. "Green skin, yellow eyes. Goblins." He sets the journal down carefully. "This is worse than I thought. That cave has been sealed for years. If goblins have moved in, there's no telling what they've been doing in there." He slides payment across the table. "You've done good work. Rest up and come talk to me when you're ready for a {yellow}real fight{/}. We need to deal with whatever's in that {location}cave{/} before it spills out into the hamlet."
steps.inProgressDialogue: "Found anything out there yet? Keep looking. Check the campsite in the forest."

data.name: The Goblin's Den
data.description: Armed with evidence of goblins from the torn journal, Garak sends you into the cave to deal with the goblin threat lurking in the tunnels beneath the Hearthstead Wilds.
data.denialDialogue: Garak shakes his head. "You need to investigate the wilds first. Come back when you've found something."
data.completedDialogue: Garak raises his mug. "The goblin is dead and the cave is a little safer. Well done."

steps.description: Speak with {npc}Garak{/} at the {location}Hearthstead Inn{/} about the {yellow}cave{/}.
steps.completionDialogue: Garak's jaw tightens. "That journal confirmed what I feared. Goblins have settled into the cave north of the forest." He pushes a rough sketch across the table. "Head back through the {location}Hearthstead Wilds{/} and keep going north until you find the {location}cave mouth{/}. The tunnels twist, so keep your bearings. Somewhere deep inside, there's a {npc}goblin{/} running things. Kill it, and the rest will scatter." He pauses. "Watch for spiders in there. The cave is thick with them."

steps.description: Find and kill the {npc}corrupted goblin{/} in the {location}Goblin's Den{/} deep within the cave.
steps.completionDialogue: The goblin crumples with a snarl, crude weapons clattering to the stone floor. Stolen supplies and gnawed bones litter its den. Whatever drew this creature here, it won't be troubling anyone else. But deeper in the tunnels, a low growl echoes off the walls. Something larger is down there.
steps.inProgressDialogue: "The goblin is still alive? Get back in there."

steps.description: Return to {npc}Garak{/} at the {location}Hearthstead Inn{/} and report the {yellow}goblin dead{/}.
steps.completionDialogue: Garak nods, visibly relieved. "One less problem. But you said you heard something deeper?" He frowns. "That cave goes further than anyone realized. I've heard stories of a {npc}beast{/} laired in the deepest tunnels. Bones, scraps, the works." He slides your payment across the table. "Rest up and get stronger. When you're ready, come talk to me about the {yellow}beast below{/}. That thing needs to be dealt with before winter drives it out looking for food."
steps.inProgressDialogue: "Is the goblin dead? Get back in there and finish the job."

data.name: The Beast Below
data.description: With the goblin dealt with, Garak reveals the greater threat: something large and dangerous lairs in the deepest chamber of the cave. Return and slay the cave bear.
data.denialDialogue: Garak looks you over. "The cave is no place for the unprepared. Deal with the goblin first, then we'll talk."
data.completedDialogue: Garak raises his mug. "The cave is clear and Hearthstead is safe. You've earned your rest."

steps.description: Speak with {npc}Garak{/} at the {location}Hearthstead Inn{/} about the {yellow}beast below{/}.
steps.completionDialogue: Garak's expression is grave. "You heard it down there. Something big has made its lair in the deepest part of that cave." He taps the table. "The {npc}cave bear{/}. Bones everywhere, from what the old miners used to say. Nobody's gone that deep in years." He meets your eyes. "You've proven yourself against goblins and spiders. This is the real test. Head back to the {location}cave{/}, push past the {location}Goblin's Den{/}, and find the {location}Bone-Littered Burrow{/} at the very end. Kill the bear. End this."

steps.description: Slay the {npc}cave bear{/} in the {location}Bone-Littered Burrow{/} at the deepest point of the cave.
steps.completionDialogue: The massive bear collapses with a shuddering roar that echoes through the cavern. Silence follows. Bones of animals and less fortunate travelers are scattered across the burrow floor. The air is thick with the stench of old kills. With the bear dead, the cave is finally safe.
steps.inProgressDialogue: "The bear still lives? You need to finish this."

steps.description: Return to {npc}Garak{/} at the {location}Hearthstead Inn{/} and report the {yellow}bear slain{/}.
steps.completionDialogue: Garak stares at you for a long moment, then breaks into a wide grin. "You actually did it." He pushes a heavy pouch across the table and clasps your hand. "The hamlet owes you a debt. The cave is clear, the forest is safe, and the farmers can sleep without worry." He releases your hand and leans back. "You've outgrown this place. The road south leads to the {location}river crossing{/}. Swim across and follow it to {location}Arindale{/}. That's where the real opportunities are." He raises his mug. "Watch your back out there. The world beyond these woods is not as forgiving."
steps.inProgressDialogue: "Is the bear dead? Don't come back until it is."
